channel.title: Posts on notamaan.xyz
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on notamaan.xyz

item.title: 😈 Scamming Scammers (Gone Wrong!) 😱
item.description:
introduction 👋

recently I was looking for side hustle opportunities to make some money on the side. During my research, I came across a great way to do this: online tutoring. Long story short, I created a profile on superprof.ie and was contacted by scammers. This is what happened next.

initial contact 📩

being desperate for money, I created an account on superprof.ie which seemed like a good platform to find students within ireland. After creating the account, did not get any students (no shit) for a while so I gave up and started to look at other avenues to make money.

item.title: 🛠️ Hacking My Router for Fun and Profit 💰
item.description:
introduction 👋

hi guys, I am back with another exciting blog.

our objectives for today:

  • root password
  • WPA2 password
  • web interface password

so recently I have been reading and watching a lot of videos on IoT hacking. An amazing channel for me is matt brown’s channel. He has a lot of good videos on how to get started and goes into quite a bit of depth.

after learning from all these resources, I decided to buy a cheap kit from temu and a cheap target router to get started with IoT hacking.

item.title: 🧑‍💻Learning C2 with Sliver🛰️
item.description:
introduction

hi guys, I hope everyone is still goin strong with their new year resolutions 😀

in this blog we will be looking at how actual command and control works by setting up and playing with a realistic(ish) environment. I will be using sliver as an example but havoc and GunnerC2 are great open source alternatives.

you can also explore some other open source ones here

installation

installing sliver is very easy, just use the following one liner to install it

item.title: 🕵️ Hunting for Leaked Secrets on Github (I Used AI) 🤖
item.description:
Disclaimer

The research presented is purely for educational purposes on already-public data and must not be used to attempt unauthorized access.

Introduction

Now that I got you to click on the article by putting “AI” in the title, here is what I did.

Gharchive is a website that basically creates frequent snapshots of all the public code pushed to github. Think of “The Wayback Machine” from archive.org but for github.

item.title: My Experience with OSCP 🐉
item.description:
Disclaimer

Hi guys, This is going to be the first post on my website and I am not really used to writing long blog posts so please excuse my writing style which may come off as a bit rough around the edges.

How it started 🚀

In this post, I will be discussing about my OSCP journey and how I managed to get my OSCP (and OSCP+) certifications in March 2025.

item.title: Moving to Ireland!
item.description:
Life Update

Hey! some of you might have noticed that I have been gone for a while.

item.title: Tunnels! Best Way to Access your Home Network from Outside 🚇🏠🌍
item.description:
Traditional Methods 🏠

One of my most cherished memories is the day I realized that my cousin, sitting in his house, could not connect to my minecraft server even after I gave him my IP address and port number! I remember thinking that all those youtube tutorials fooled me into thinking that I could play minecraft with my cousin without him coming to my place. It was just too good to be true! Now I realise that those tutorials were only for people playing on the same network.
